result:
The refactor is complete and verified. All 4 tests pass unmodified, and no subscript access to the config remains anywhere in `reportgen/`.

Summary of changes:

**`reportgen/config.py`** тАФ Replaced the `DEFAULTS` dict with a frozen dataclass:
- `Config` is `@dataclass(frozen=True)` with typed fields `title: str`, `output_format: str`, `max_rows: int`, `include_summary: bool`, carrying the same defaults as before.
- `load_config(path)` now validates against the known field names (via `fields(Config)`), raising `ValueError` naming any unknown key, then constructs `Config(**raw)` so JSON values override defaults just as before.

**`reportgen/formatter.py`** тАФ Switched `config["..."]` to attribute access (`config.output_format`, `config.title`, `config.include_summary`).

**`reportgen/loader.py`** тАФ Switched `config["max_rows"]` to `config.max_rows`.

`main.py` needed no changes тАФ it just threads the config object through. Validation happens naturally because unknown keys now go through `Config(**raw)` only after the explicit check, and `frozen=True` makes the config immutable as required.
